## Introduction

### You are starting a course for a UAV remote pilot in subcategory A1/A3 of the open category. Good luck!

The course is addressed to people of over 14 years of age. It is designed to prepare you to conduct basic visual line of sight flights. You will learn where and how you may fly safely in accordance with the rules so as not to endanger your life and the lives of other people nearby.
The certificate which you will receive after completing the course and passing the exam is valid across the European Union (as well as in Iceland, Lichtenstein, Norway and Switzerland).

**If you have ever piloted a drone – remember that new EU regulation has been in force since 31 December 2020!**

---
## Provisions of law

### Provisions of law and institutions which a UAV remote pilot must know

A UAV remote pilot must know that the compliance with aviation regulations in the European Union is supervised by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA).
In Poland, aviation supervision is exercised by the Polish Civil Aviation Authority (Urząd Lotnictwa Cywilnego, ULC), and our airspace is managed by the Polish Air Navigation Services Agency (PANSA, or Polska Agencja Żeglugi Powietrznej, PAŻP).

|           ![[Pasted image 20251214190104.png]]           | ![](data:image/png;base64,) | ![](data:image/png;base64,) |
| :------------------------------------------------------: | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
|    **EASA - European Union Aviation Safety Agency:**     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         **ULC - Polish Civil Aviation Authority:**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  **PAŻP - Polish Air Navigation Services Agency:**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Oversees compliance with aviation regulations in the EU. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Responsible for aviation supervision in Poland.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Manages the Polish airspace.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |

A UAV remote pilot should also know applicable rules. An extract of these rules is the main part of this training, but we also encourage you to read:

- COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2019/947 of 24 May 2019 on the rules and procedures for the operation of unmanned aircraft;
- COMMISSION DELEGATED REGULATION (EU) 2019/945 of 12 March 2019 on unmanned aircraft systems and on third-country operators of unmanned aircraft systems;
- Guidelines of the President of the Polish Civil Aviation Authority.


[Complete set of current legal regulations](https://ulc.gov.pl/pl/drony/informacje-ogolne/akty-prawne)


### Flight categories – what are they for and how do they relate to me?

The European Union Aviation Safety Agency has developed common guidelines to ensure free movement of drones and equal footing for all UAV operators across the European Union.**The division into categories is based on a flight risk analysis which takes into account primarily the mass and specification of the UAV and the character of the intended operation.**

The following categories are distinguished:

- **OPEN CATEGORY** – If you are just starting your drone adventure and intend to pilot a UAV with take-off mass of up to 25 kg within your Visual Line Of Sight (VLOS) and at a height of maximum 120 m – this means you will be flying in the **open category**. It is divided into the subcategories (A1, A2, A3), with each having its own set of requirements.
  
- **SPECIFIC CATEGORY** – medium-risk flights (operations) which require permission of the President of the Polish Civil Aviation Authority (apart from VLOS, also BVLOS – Beyond Visual Line of Sight; flights at a height greater than 120 m) or submission of an operational declaration in accordance with a national standard scenario (NSTS) or a Union standard scenario (STS). It also requires relevant pilot privileges to conduct such operations (e.g. flights at a height greater than 120 m).
  
- **CERTIFIED CATEGORY** - high-risk flights (e.g. carriage of dangerous goods, persons, flights over assemblies of people) which require certified equipment and personnel.

| **Open category:**                                                                                 | **Special category:**                                      | **Certified category:**                            |
| :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------- |
| - Low risk<br>- Drones up to 25 kg<br>- Flights within visual line of sight up to a height of 120m | - Medium risk<br>- Required consent or notification to ULC | - High risk<br>- Certified equipment and personnel |

**What is the purpose of this division?**

So that we can fly safely and in accordance with the rules and intended paths – just like aeroplan pilots.
UAV operation categories are based on a risk analysis. In principle. Open category operations are the safest ones, that is if you comply with the rules. Remember that there are other aircraft in the air, and people, animals and buildings on the ground.

> [!abstract] Remember
>**You cannot fly in the open category above assemblies of people!**
>Subcategory A1 – only temporary flying over people is permitted (if they suddenly appear within the flight route),
>Subcategory A2 – you can fly near people; minimum horizontal distance from people is 30 m (or 5 m, if the drone has a flight speed limit function),
>Subcategory A3 – you can only fly far from people, buildings and other terrain obstacles.

### Drone classes and subcategories in the OPEN CATEGORY

Now look at your UAV. See what designation it has – C0, C1, C2, C3, C4.

If there is letter C followed by a digit from 0 to 4 on your drone and in the instruction manual, then your drone meets the EU standard requirements and is categorized in class C, which permits UAV flights in the open category.
As you can see, the class designation on your drone is relevant to what, if any, competence you will need to fly it.

| Sub - category | EU class | Basic flight rules                                                                                                                                | Weight | Max. flight speed | Registration                                                | Training and exam          |
| -------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| A1             | C0       | overflight of persons – only if necessary, but not over assemblies of people                                                                      | <250 g | <19m/s            | YES, if it is able to capture personal data (e.g. a camera) | NO                         |
| A1             | C1       | overflight of persons – only if necessary, but not over assemblies of people                                                                      | <900 g | <19 m/s           | YES                                                         | YES, on-line (in progress) |
| A3             | C3       | flights away from persons, not over assemblies of people, min. 150 m away from urban, industrial and other areas, max. size up to 3 m             | <25 kg | n/a               | YES                                                         | YES, on-line (in progress) |
| A3             | C4       | model aircraft – no automatic modes; flights away from persons, not over assemblies of people, min. 150 m from urban, industrial and other areas. | <25 kg | n/a               | YES                                                         | YES, on-line (in progress) |

>[!info] Fun fact
>The students of the Technical University in Delft (The Netherlands) have constructed a drone-ambulance, equipped with medical supplies, a camera and a speaker. This allows a professional paramedic to instruct people at the accident scene. The Dutch estimate that car accident mortality can decline by almost 80%.

### What to do when your drone does not have the C designation? Are you allowed to fly it?

**Yes – you are allowed to fly it.** The absence of the designation most likely means that your drone was manufactured before 1 January 2024 or the manufacturer did not assign a class to your UAV.
Consult your instruction manual or designation on your drone to see the maximum take-off mass of your UAV, if:

1. The drone has the maximum take-off mass of less than 250 g:
    - the training which you have just started is not obligatory, but we strongly insist that you complete it – for your own safety and for the safety of other airspace users;
    - you may fly over uninvolved persons with particular caution and limiting the overflight time to the minimum;
    - you cannot fly over assemblies of people;
    - Operator's Manual: Carefully review the drone's operator's manual for detailed instructions and safety guidelines.
2. Drones 250g and above (but less than 25kg):
    - Maintain Distance: Maintain a safe distance from people;
    - 150m Horizontal Separation: Keep a horizontal distance of 150 meters from residential, commercial, industrial, or recreational areas;
    - Operator's Manual: Carefully review the drone's operator's manual for detailed instructions and safety guidelines;
    - Mandatory Training and Exam: Complete the provided training course and pass the final exam to obtain an A3 sub-category drone operator license.
3. The drone has take-off mass up to 900 g:  
    - you must complete this course and pass the final test – you will then be allowed to fly a drone in subcategory A1;
    - you may fly over uninvolved persons with particular caution and limiting the overflight time to the minimum;
    - you cannot fly over assemblies of people;
    - you must use a drone with a C1 class designation assigned by the manufacturer for the flight operation.
4. The drone has take-off mass up to 4 kg:  
    - you must complete this course and pass the test – you will then be allowed to fly a drone in subcategory A3;
    - you may fly over uninvolved persons with particular caution and limiting the overflight time to the minimum;
    - you cannot fly over assemblies of people;
    - if you need to fly within 50 metres [or lower] from uninvolved persons – complete the course and pass the exam for subcategory A2.
5. The drone has take-off mass up to 25 kg:  
    - you must complete this course and pass the test – you will then be allowed to fly your drone in subcategory A3;
    - you must maintain your UAV at a horizontal distance of at least 150 metres from residential, commercial, industrial or recreational areas;
    - you cannot fly over assemblies of people;

> [!abstract] Remember
>  Pursuant to EU regulations, an ‘assembly of people’ is when the density of people within a given area prevents their free movement (e.g., during a concert, mass).

### Geographical zones – basic information

In every airspace there are various flight safety zones or actions that require segregation from air traffic. They are the so-called airspace structures.
Geographical zones are equivalent to the zones in manned aviation CTR, MCTR, ATZ, TRA, TSA, R, P, D, MRT and many new defined solely with UAVs in mind.

**A geographical zone is an area that applies only and exclusively to operations conducted with unmanned aerial systems.** They are an excellent and flexible tool for defining UAV flight conditions within a given area, and create an air space structure for drones. A geographical zone contains limitations or absolute prohibitions on UAV flights. These zones in our country are defined by PANSA.

Why does PANSA define such zones?

1. For safety, e.g. the safety of aerodromes, radars, strategic facilities, etc.
2. For the protection of privacy.
3. For the protection of government (e.g. national parks).
  
![](data:image/png;base64,)

Example illustration of geographical zones

> [!danger] Uwaga
> Your certificate will be valid within the entire European Union, but if you are planning a mission outside Poland, remember to check local geographical zones (on the EASA website). Each country sets its own zones!  
Failure to observe local regulations may lead to hazardous situations, and severe penalties for the pilot.

>[!info] Fun Fact
>After a drone operated by tourists fell into the protected hot springs at the Yellowstone Park, the United States banned drone flights over all US National Parks.

### Geographical zones – types and rules


| Zone name  | Zone description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Rules for drones up to 900g                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Rules for drones from 900g but up to 25 kg                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **DRA-P**  | **DRone Airspace Prohibited** - a prohibited geographical zone, e.g., military aviation routes [MRT]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Zone prohibited for unmanned aerial systems, where operations involving systems of unmanned aerial vehicles cannot be executed, except for operations by the Armed Forces Operational Commander, Commander-in-Chief of the Military Police, Chief of the Polish Armed Forces Aviation Services Command, Chief of the Internal Security Agency, Chief of the Foreign Intelligence Agency, Commander-in-Chief of the Police, Commander-in-Chief of the Border Guard, Commander-in-Chief of the Fire Service, Chief of the National Revenue Administration, Chief of the Central Anti-Corruption Bureau, Commander-in-Chief of the State Protection Service or the Chief Environmental Protection Inspector – under the conditions set out by the Agency. | \|\|                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| **DRA-R**  | **DRone Airspace Restricted** - a restricted geographical zone                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | A zone restricted for systems of unmanned aerial vehicles, wherein the operations involving the systems of unmanned aerial vehicles can be executed at the approval of and under the conditions set out by the Agency or authorized entity, at the request of which the geographical zone is to be set out, if the Agency or authorized entity are able to determine the probability of granting approval of the geographical zone administrator for flying within a given zone.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | \|\|                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| **DRA-RH** | **DRone Airspace Restricted High** - a restricted geographical zone where the likelihood of obtaining permission for a drone flight is high, e.g., more than 6 km from an aerodrome                                                                                                                                                                               | \|\|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Flying UAVs with a take-off mass of up to 25 kg is possible up to the height of 100 m AGL (ground level) without coordinating with PANSA. Open-category flights above that height are impossible. |
| **DRA-RM** | **DRone Airspace Restricted Medium** - a restricted geographical zone where the likelihood of obtaining permission for a drone flight is medium, e.g. zones between 1 and 6 km from aerodromes, zones above 500 m from protected facilities                                                                                                                       | It does not require permission of the zone administrator. Flights up to the height of 30 m AGL for a pilot with A1/A3 qualifications.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Flight with a drone exhibiting a take-off mass exceeding 900 g executed by a pilot with A1/A3 qualifications are impossible                                                                       |
| **DRA-RL** | **DRone Airspace Restricted Low** - a restricted geographical zone where the likelihood of obtaining permission for a drone flight is low, e.g., a zone within 1 km from an aerodrome, <500 m from protected facilities, zones over military training grounds, temporary restricted zones (e.g. restricted by the Polish Border Guard), zones over National Parks | Flights are allowed only with the permission of and on the conditions established by the zone operator. DRA-RL-CTR flights are impossible in the open category.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | \|\|                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| **DRA-T**  | **DRone Airspace Technical** - a restricted geographical zone where PANSA indicates technical conditions that the UAV must meet                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Flights only after meeting the technical requirements established by PANSA.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | \|\|                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| **DRA-U**  | **DRone Airspace U-Space** - a geographical zone wherein UAV flights may be conducted only with the support of verified services provided in the zone concerned and under conditions indicated by PANSA                                                                                                                                                           | Flights are allowed only with the use of specific services (provided in the zone concerned) and under conditions defined by PANSA.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | \|\|                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| **DRA-I**  | **DRone Airspace Information** - an informative geographical zone which contains data which is necessary for ensuring UAV flight safety, including navigation warnings                                                                                                                                                                                            | Flights do not require permission. Information and warnings important/relevant to the UAV pilot when flying in the geographical zone.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | \|\|                                                                                                                                                                                              |

> [!danger] Note!
> When checking geographical zones, remember that an airspace changes dynamically. New zones may appear at any location and time. The flexible ones can be deactivated or, in other words, decommissioned. The UAV pilot shall be responsible for familiarizing with current restrictions. This is why you should remember to notify PANSA on the intended flight prior to each operation – through the teleinformatics system, as defined by the Agency (details on the PANSA website): [https://www.pansa.pl/dronetower](https://www.pansa.pl/dronetower).  
If you fail to observe the principles of a geographical zone you fly in, you are facing a legal sanction stipulated by the regulations, including prison time!

### Controlled and uncontrolled airspace

Geographical zones are defined horizontally over a defined area, but what about the airspace above us? **That space is also appropriately divided into classes**, depending on the type of service provided to pilots by competent entities (in Poland – PANSA).
Classes from A to E mean controlled airspaces. In Poland, we have mainly class C airspace (in some places also D), in which PANSA provides:

- ATC – Air Traffic Control,
- AIS – Aeronautical Information Service,
- ALRS – Alerting Service.

However, of interest to remote pilots (since this is the largest airspace above Poland) are mainly uncontrolled classes – in Poland, it is only class G. In this airspace, PANSA does not provide the so-called separation, i.e. Air Traffic Control. However, the Air Traffic Service (ATS) in the form of information service (FIS) is at our disposal.
